description_long: This object is a ground-mounted solar panel installation situated on a patch of grassy terrain. The solar panel array consists of six rectangular photovoltaic modules arranged in a 2x3 grid, supported by a single vertical metal pole anchored to a concrete base. The panel surface is dark, likely composed of glass-covered silicon cells, and is framed by a metallic border. The pole and base appear to be made of galvanized steel or aluminum, providing structural stability. The panel is tilted at an angle to optimize sunlight capture, and no visible electrical cabling or inverter housing is present. The installation is typical for small-scale solar energy generation in urban parks, roadside verges, or community spaces.
description: A ground-mounted solar panel array with six modules on a metal pole and concrete base, set on a grassy patch. The panels are tilted for optimal sunlight capture and framed in metal.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full face of the solar panel array and the supporting pole centered on a grassy mound.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the panel's tilt and the thickness of the metal frame, with the pole and base visible.
description_view_2: Direct rear view of the solar panel array, showing the back of the modules and the supporting structure.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view, emphasizing the panel's tilt and the shadow cast on the grass.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 30 degrees, facing away from the pole, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: pole-mounted, supported by a single vertical metal pole and concrete base
attribute_array_configuration: 2x3 grid of solar panels mounted as a single array
